concrete.label: Concrete
concrete.source_notes: P.2040-3 Annex 1 Table 3 row 'Concrete'. Used for building shells (load-bearing walls, slabs).

glass.label: Glass
glass.source_notes: P.2040-3 Annex 1 Table 3 row 'Glass'. Used for window panes (single-pane). Modern IRR / low-emissivity glass has 10-30 dB extra penetration loss not captured by this row — tag separately when MapBiomas/OSM identifies modern facades.

metal.label: Metal
metal.source_notes: P.2040-3 Annex 1 Table 3 row 'Metal' — modelled as a near-PEC (sigma=1e7 S/m, epsilon_r=1). At 28-60 GHz the skin depth is < 1 micron so reflection is effectively total; we keep finite sigma to avoid singularities in the Fresnel equations inside Mitsuba.

vegetation.label: Vegetation (foliage)
vegetation.source_notes: NOT in P.2040-3 Annex 1 Table 3. P.2040-3 defers to P.833 for vegetation attenuation (RET model). The values here are an engineering approximation tuned so Mitsuba scenes with tagged tree canopies produce ~ 0.4-0.6 dB/m specific attenuation at 28 GHz, matching P.833 RET in-leaf for typical urban deciduous canopies. Replace with a per-canopy fit when MapBiomas land-cover classes are wired up (Tijolo 5).
